detail: Initialize Supabase project and create waitlist database schema
Implement /api/waitlist POST endpoint and connect WaitlistForm
Set up Resend email service and send branded confirmation emails
Implement real-time webhook notifications for waitlist signups
Implement admin authentication with Next.js middleware and session cookies

Using Next.js 16 App Router built-in features: metadata API for SEO (no react-helmet needed), convention-based metadata files (icon.png, opengraph-image.png), dynamic route handlers for sitemap/robots. React-GA4 library for GA4 integration with custom event tracking. All implementations leverage server components where possible, client components only for analytics initialization. TypeScript for type safety across analytics helper functions. Environment variables for GA4 Measurement ID to support dev/prod separation.

Next.js 16 App Router with Server Components for SEO and performance. Supabase PostgreSQL for data persistence (waitlist + orders tables). Stripe for payment processing with CAD currency. Resend for transactional emails (domain verification required for production). Google Analytics 4 for event tracking. Slack webhooks for real-time notifications. TypeScript throughout for type safety. Tailwind CSS v4 for styling consistency with existing brand. Server Actions avoided in favor of explicit API routes for better debugging and Vercel edge compatibility.

Using Next.js 15 App Router with Server Actions and Route Handlers. Lightweight session-based auth (httpOnly cookies) instead of NextAuth to minimize dependencies. TanStack Table v8 for robust client-side table features. Supabase aggregations for stats to reduce client-side computation. Papaparse for CSV generation (well-tested, handles edge cases). All styling with Tailwind CSS v4 matching existing design system.

**Stack:** Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Supabase (PostgreSQL), Resend (transactional email), React Email (email templates). **Architecture:** API route pattern (/app/api/waitlist/route.ts) for backend logic, client-side form with progressive enhancement, webhook notifications as non-blocking async operation. **Security:** Row-Level Security on Supabase with public insert-only policy, server-side validation in API route, env vars for all secrets. **Deployment:** Vercel with environment variables for production secrets.

Build ArcticRuff from waitlist to revenue in 4 phases: (1) capture demand via Supabase-backed waitlist with automated notifications, (2) enable internal operations with admin dashboard, (3) monetize with Stripe checkout, and (4) drive organic traffic with SEO and analytics. Each project builds on the previous, enabling rapid iteration from MVP to full e-commerce.
